summary: Adds a format-check CI job that installs gofumpt at a pinned version (v0.9.2) and verifies all Go files conform to gofumpt formatting. All 22 existing Go files were reformatted with whitespace-only changes (import reordering, alignment, function signature formatting). Documentation in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md was updated with a new format-check section, DAG update, and env var description. No existing CI jobs were modified.

code_quality_checklist.item: CI job matches lint job structure (needs, if, SHA pins)
Go file changes are whitespace-only (gofumpt formatting)

security_checklist.item: Actions use SHA-pinned references (not mutable tags)
No supply chain risk from mutable action tags

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
format-check job is independent gate (not added to other jobs' needs)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Job exits 1 on formatting violations with diff output

issues_found.description: Documentation step table shows incorrect gofumpt install command. The table lists `go install gofumpt@${{ env.GOFUMPT_VERSION }}` but the actual CI step uses the full module path `go install mvdan.cc/gofumpt@${{ env.GOFUMPT_VERSION }}`. A developer copy-pasting the documented command would get a Go module resolution error.
issues_found.fix: Change `go install gofumpt@${{ env.GOFUMPT_VERSION }}` to `go install mvdan.cc/gofumpt@${{ env.GOFUMPT_VERSION }}` in the step table.
issues_found.evidence: | 3 | `go install gofumpt@${{ env.GOFUMPT_VERSION }}` | Installs gofumpt at the pinned version (`v0.9.2`) |

issues_found.description: Task 3.1 requires a note that gofumpt is a strict superset of gofmt, but the format-check documentation section does not include this note. The issue description also states this motivation explicitly. This context helps developers understand why gofumpt was chosen over gofmt.
issues_found.fix: Add a sentence to the opening paragraph, e.g.: 'gofumpt is a strict superset of gofmt that enforces additional formatting rules (e.g. removing unnecessary blank lines, grouping imports consistently).'
issues_found.evidence:
Verifies all Go files conform to gofumpt formatting (CC-0053). Detects non-conforming files
and prints a unified diff showing the required changes, so developers can identify and fix
formatting issues without guessing.

next_steps: Fix the gofumpt module path in the docs step table (mvdan.cc/gofumpt)
Add note that gofumpt is a strict superset of gofmt to the format-check docs section
